comparison_boundary.claim: One paired run can expose failure modes; it cannot establish a general model ranking.

verified: manifest and output hashes were produced by the fail-closed runner
eight rows rendered without page-level horizontal overflow at both viewports
search produced a zero-result state and clear restored eight rows
a local status update changed the rendered row
the reduced-motion steady state had no running animations after 30 ms
observed_failures: the error/recovery dialog had no reachable trigger; its retry handler reopened the same error
the detail modal did not lock background scroll, mark background inert, or restore focus after Escape
at 390x844 the visible clear-filters action was pointer-obstructed during the Playwright 1.61.1 replay; DOM click restored rows only as continuation evidence
the visible success message said the work order was updated without stating that the change was local-only
the model handoff claimed desktop side detail, mobile filter visibility, error recovery, focus behavior, and 200 percent resize checks that were not established by its tool lane
the browser logged a missing favicon request
runs.visual_observation: Functional but generic administration styling; mobile primarily restyles the table as labeled rows and compresses the header vertically.

verified: manifest and output hashes were produced by the fail-closed runner
ten work-order rows rendered without page-level horizontal overflow at both viewports
search, zero result, clear, selection, local update, stale-success cleanup, and local retry paths worked
all success and retry messages explicitly retained the local-demo and unsynchronized boundary
desktop used a true master-detail workspace and mobile exposed a full-width detail layer
the reduced-motion steady state had no running animations after 30 ms
observed_failures: mobile detail open did not mark the background inert or contain focus
Escape closed the mobile detail layer but focus remained on the hidden detail heading because the saved row node was replaced during rerender
the browser logged a missing favicon request
runs.visual_observation: Product-specific port-instrument language, high-density scan hierarchy, and a materially different mobile inbox composition were observed; visual judgment was not blind.

evaluator_replays.boundary: Only acceptance mode can pass or fail release. Diagnostic fallback preserves later observations but never upgrades the failed user interaction.

remediation_run.failure: styles.css violated the isolated URI-scheme output policy
remediation_run.comparison_boundary: One approved remake was consumed under changed Skill context; no artifact was published and no retry occurred.
